title: EPF vs mutual funds: Why EPFO says your provident fund should remain foundation of retirement planning
summary: EPFO has urged salaried employees not to withdraw EPF savings for mutual funds, highlighting EPF's retirement, pension and insurance benefits. Here's how EPF differs from mutual funds, key features, tax benefits and practical investment takeaways for long-term financial planning.

title: Can an insurer reject your claim over a tax issue? Consumer panel answers
summary: A Rajasthan consumer commission has reportedly ruled that insurers cannot reject an otherwise valid claim merely because of an unrelated tax irregularity. The decision underlines that the reason for claim repudiation must have a direct link with the insured loss.

title: ETF split FAQs: Why fund houses are splitting units and how investors benefit
summary: A split reduces the per-unit price of an ETF and increases the number of units an investor owns, while the total value of the investment remains the same. It is not a taxable event and tax applies only when the investor sells the ETF units.

title: Global diversification matters more than ever, says Marcellus' Saurabh Mukherjea
summary: Marcellus Investment Managers' founder and CIO Saurabh Mukherjea argues that portfolios anchored solely to domestic markets may no longer be enough.

title: CGHS pensioner card goes paperless via Bhavishya Portal, making healthcare access for retirees faster and simpler
summary: The Centre has introduced a paperless CGHS pensioner card application process through the Bhavishya Portal for retiring central government employees. The move enables auto-filled applications, PAN-based verification, online payment and faster access to CGHS healthcare benefits after retirement.

title: Revised ITR cannot take away capital loss carry-forward if original return was filed on time: ITAT
summary: A Bengaluru taxpayer won relief after the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal held that filing a revised ITR does not extinguish the right to carry forward capital losses if the original return was filed within the prescribed due date.

title: Pay income tax online: Here's everything you need to know about the 5 payment modes under the e-Pay Tax facility
summary: The e-Pay Tax facility allows taxpayers to pay direct taxes online quickly and securely without visiting a tax office. It supports both pre-login and post-login payments through multiple payment modes, offering flexibility and easy access to challans and payment records.

title: NPS rule change: Pension funds liable for third-party lapses under new PFRDA rules
summary: PFRDA has amended NPS regulations to make pension funds accountable for lapses by third-party entities engaged to provide subscriber services. The regulator has also introduced a Regulatory Sandbox framework to encourage innovation while safeguarding investors.

title: First-time home buyers: 5 mistakes that can increase your EMI burden
summary: First-time home buyers often make costly loan mistakes that increase EMIs and overall repayment burden. Experts explain how tenure, down payment, credit score, hidden costs and borrowing limits impact home loan affordability, helping buyers make smarter financial decisions.

title: Global Market: BNP Paribas Q2 profit jumps 33% on strong trading, retail rebound; beats estimates
summary: BNP Paribas reported a better-than-expected 33% rise in second-quarter net profit to €4.35 billion, driven by strong equity trading, a recovery in retail banking and gains from its revamped insurance partnership with Ageas.

title: Indonesia's Danantara plans dollar bond sale worth at least $500 mln, sources say

title: Global Market: ECB seen holding rates steady while keeping September hike option open
summary: The European Central Bank is expected to maintain current interest rates at its upcoming policy meeting. summary: Indo MIM has raised Rs 1,141 crore from anchor investors ahead of its Rs 3,811 crore IPO, drawing participation from global institutions including BlackRock and domestic investors such as Prashant Jain-backed 3P India. title: Tesla Q2 Highlights: Bigger AI Bets, Thinner Margins, Mixed Results
summary: Tesla beat revenue estimates in the second quarter but missed earnings expectations as margins weakened and free cash flow turned negative. Investors remain focused on its aggressive AI and autonomous technology investments.
